time: Before the evening of the murder
subject: Livia Marr
action: died from a sleeping compound overdose
context: Her death preceded Aldric's murder and was initially thought possibly suicidal

subject: Livia Marr
action: spread early reports about Livia's death
context: Suspected suicide motivated by guilt over her confessions; information passed through informal networks

subject: Livia Marr
action: caused her first husband's death and had been paying a blackmailer
context: Livia privately admitted this to Aldric before her death; she refused to name the blackmailer in person

subject: Livia Marr's letter
action: arrived and may name the extortionist
context: Final letter arrived via evening post; Aldric began reading it aloud, then decided to finish alone

subject: Dr. Silas Wren
action: departed from Aldric's study
context: Left Aldric alone to finish reading Livia's letter; encountered unfamiliar young man near estate gates

subject: unknown young man / Niall Corven
action: loitering near the estate
context: Rough-looking stranger seen near gates; later identified as Niall Corven who admitted visiting secretly; connected to Mistress Sable

subject: Aldric Vale
action: heard Aldric's voice from the study saying he did not wish to be disturbed
context: Evidence depends on whether this was live voice or recording; later confirmed voice-recording cabinet existed

subject: Rowan Vale
action: staying at the inn and involved with an unidentified woman
context: Had been staying at Blue Heron inn; secretly married to Elia Moss; feared disinheritance due to concealed marriage

subject: Maren Vale
action: claimed to have seen Aldric alive after dinner
context: Initially strengthened impression Aldric survived late into evening; later retracted admission that she only went near study while stealing money and did not actually see him

time: During her visit to steal money
subject: ceremonial glass-bladed knife
action: no longer on the hall display
context: Made the timing of its removal important to the investigation; Maren used this as pretext for her presence near study

subject: Rowan Vale
action: claims he wore heavy boots that night rather than shoes matching the incriminating prints
context: Footprints near the study window implicated him; his defense is footwear discrepancy

subject: Jonas Kett
action: denied making the telephone call summoning Dr. Wren back
context: Someone called Wren to return; Kett denies responsibility

time: Later that night, after receiving telephone summons
subject: Dr. Silas Wren
action: returned to the house
context: A telephone message appeared to summon Wren back to the house

subject: Aldric Vale
action: found Aldric dead in the study
context: The study door was locked, and the scene showed signs of struggle

subject: ceremonial knife
action: identified the ceremonial glass-bladed knife from the hall display as the murder weapon
context: The knife came from the hallway display, indicating opportunity to take it

location: study garden window / outside the garden window
subject: garden window and footprints
action: noted the open garden window and documented suspicious footprints
context: Along with suspicious footprints outside the window; footprints appeared staged-looking, matching Rowan's shoes

subject: study interior
action: chair had been shifted and objects arranged differently on the table
context: Details noted upon examination of the room

subject: Rowan Vale
action: went missing after the murder
context: Financially dependent on Aldric; had quarreled with him recently

time: After Aldric learned of the marriage
subject: Elia Moss
action: was dismissed from service
context: Lost her position as parlour maid after the secret marriage was discovered

subject: Rowan Vale
action: treated as the likely murderer
context: Based on motive (fear of disinheritance from secret marriage), disappearance, recent quarrel, and shoe evidence matching footprints

subject: Aldric's will
action: explained the terms of Aldric's will and financial arrangements
context: Clarified who benefits from Aldric's death and how Rowan, Maren, Dame Perrin, and others stand financially

subject: the Vale family / Aldric's papers
action: spoke about the family's financial dependence on Aldric and concealed self-interested conduct
context: Her evidence mainly clarified financial motives within the household; her evasiveness and concern with money made her suspicious

subject: Captain Orven Blaise
action: was emotionally guarded due to feelings for Maren Vale
context: His restraint and awkwardness created suspicion, though his evidence was mostly observational

subject: Mistress Iona Sable
action: asked Dr. Wren about addiction, rare poisons, and paralyzing toxins
context: Her line of questioning created suspicion around herself during the inquiry; later indicated these questions related to a private concern about a troubled young man connected to her past

subject: Niall Corven
action: had a history of drug use
context: Part of his character profile during the inquiry

subject: Niall Corven / Footprint evidence
action: corroborated Niall Corven's movements, appearance, and poor boots
context: Helps distinguish him from the person whose prints appeared near Aldric's study window and weakens the theory that he made those marks
witnesses: The barmaid at the Copper Finch

subject: voice-recording cabinet
action: confirmed that Aldric had recently obtained a voice-recording cabinet
context: Made the heard voice potentially misleading as to Aldric's actual presence

subject: various case subjects
action: repeated reports about Livia Marr, Aldric Vale, Maren, Rowan, possible blackmail, and village movements
context: Some reflected real observation, but much was speculative, biased, or secondhand; village gossip about blackmail and Livia's confession circulated

subject: steward
action: may have listened at the door
context: The steward had a furtive past and may have listened at the door
